verdict_reasoning: CRISP1 (cysteine-rich secretory protein 1) is a secreted protein belonging to the CRISP/antigen 5/pathogenesis-related 1 (CAP) family. It is secreted from cells and binds to the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane via stable, non-covalent interactions with membrane lipids and/or membrane proteins. CRISP1 has been documented to associate with the cell surface and extracellular matrix, where it can interact with integrins and other cell-surface receptors. This stable association with the outer face of the PM via its own lipid-binding and protein-binding mechanisms qualifies it as surface-accessible by the recruitment test—it reaches and remains on the outer leaflet through its inherent biochemical properties, not merely as cargo in transit.
